title: Pilosella stenosoma
intro: Pilosella stenosoma, also known as Alpine Hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, Pilosella stenosoma has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Pilosella stenosoma is often used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into creams and ointments for topical applications.
taxonomy: Pilosella stenosoma is a member of the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as false dandelions. The species Pilosella stenosoma is part of a larger group of plants known as the Cichorieae tribe. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and current scientific understanding.
